question: Kapil Dev's famous 175* against Zimbabwe in the 1983 World Cup was not televised because of a strike at which broadcaster?
fact: No footage of Kapil Dev's unbeaten 175 exists because BBC technicians were on strike during the match.

question: Which famous painting was stolen from the Louvre in 1911 and remained missing for more than two years?
fact: The theft helped transform the Mona Lisa into one of the world's most famous paintings.

question: Which cricketer is the only player to have scored a century before lunch on the first day of a Test match?
fact: Victor Trumper achieved this feat against England at Old Trafford in 1902.

question: Which Indian city was once nicknamed the Leningrad of India because of its strong communist influence?
fact: Kolkata was one of the major centres of communist politics in India during the twentieth century.

question: The title of George Orwell's novel 1984 is widely believed to have been derived by reversing the last two digits of which year?
fact: Orwell completed the manuscript in 1948 and likely reversed the final two digits for the title.

question: Which Indian cricketer was dismissed hit wicket in both innings of the same Test match?
fact: Lala Amarnath remains one of the rare players to be hit wicket in both innings of a Test.

question: The logo of which automobile manufacturer is commonly interpreted as a propeller although that explanation is partly a later marketing myth?
fact: BMW's roundel originated from Bavarian colours and only later became associated with aircraft propellers.

question: Which country hosted the famous 1972 World Chess Championship match between Bobby Fischer and Boris Spassky?
fact: The match was played in Reykjavik and became one of the defining sporting events of the Cold War.

question: Which tennis player was nicknamed The Crocodile?
fact: Yannick Noah remains the last French man to win the French Open singles title.

question: Which football club won the first edition of the European Cup in 1956?
fact: Real Madrid won the first five editions of the European Cup.

question: Only one cricketer represented both India and England in international cricket. His grandson later became one of India's most recognizable public figures. Who is the grandson?
fact: Iftikhar Ali Khan Pataudi played for both England and India. His grandson is actor Saif Ali Khan.

question: In 1911 Bundy Manufacturing International Time Recording Tabulating Machine Company and Computing Scale Company merged to form CTR. What was CTR renamed in 1924?
fact: CTR became International Business Machines or IBM in 1924.

question: Haji Mastan married Shahjahan Begum because she resembled which legendary actress he admired?
fact: Mastan was reportedly fascinated by Madhubala after watching her films and married a lookalike.

question: Demand for photos of Jennifer Lopez's green dress at the 2000 Grammys directly led to the launch of which Google product in 2001?
fact: The overwhelming demand for images of the dress inspired Google to create Image Search.

question: What common cricketing distinction links Inzamam-ul-Haq Sadagoppan Ramesh and Virat Kohli?
fact: All three took a wicket with the first ball they bowled in international cricket.

question: Founded under a banyan tree in Bombay during the 1850s what institution later became one of India's most important financial organizations?
fact: The Bombay Stock Exchange traces its origins to traders gathering under a banyan tree near Bombay Town Hall.

question: Which two Indian legends inflicted Don Bradman's only hit-wicket and only stumped dismissals in Test cricket?
optionA: Kapil Dev and Bishan Singh Bedi
fact: Bradman was stumped by Lala Amarnath and hit wicket off Vinoo Mankad.

question: What was unusual about the date on which Jason Gillespie scored his unbeaten 201 as a nightwatchman against Bangladesh?
optionD: It was his final Test victory
fact: Jason Gillespie scored his famous double century on his birthday 19 April 2006.

question: Ajay Bhatt led the development of which technology standard that revolutionized PC connectivity?
fact: Universal Serial Bus was designed to simplify device connectivity and became the global standard.

question: Newton Heath LYR Football Club is known today by what name?
fact: Newton Heath changed its name to Manchester United in 1902.

question: Which famous Indian was once offered the post of Prime Minister of India by both Jawaharlal Nehru and later Indira Gandhi but declined on both occasions?
fact: JRD Tata was reportedly approached more than once for a political role but preferred to remain in industry.

question: Which chess world champion never lost a World Championship match and retired while still champion?
fact: Bobby Fischer never defended his title after winning it in 1972 and thus never lost a championship match.

question: Which automobile company derives its name from the Latin translation of its founder's surname meaning listen?
fact: August Horch translated Horch meaning listen into Latin and created Audi.

question: Who was the first batsman to be given out by a television umpire in international cricket?
fact: Sachin Tendulkar became the first player dismissed via third umpire decision in 1992.

question: Which novel begins with the line Call me Ishmael?
optionC: The Old Man and the Sea
fact: Moby Dick opens with one of the most famous first lines in literature.

question: Which country was the first to issue a postage stamp?
fact: The Penny Black issued in 1840 was the world's first adhesive postage stamp.

question: Which football club's stadium is popularly known as The Theatre of Dreams?
fact: Sir Bobby Charlton famously nicknamed Old Trafford The Theatre of Dreams.

question: Which Indian state was the first to be formed on a linguistic basis after independence?
fact: Andhra Pradesh was created in 1953 following the movement led by Potti Sriramulu.

question: Which company originally developed the video game character Sonic the Hedgehog?
fact: Sonic was created by Sega as a mascot to rival Nintendo's Mario.

question: Who was the first player to score a double century in One Day International cricket?
fact: Sachin Tendulkar scored 200 not out against South Africa in 2010.

question: Which famous scientist worked as a patent clerk before publishing his groundbreaking papers in 1905?
fact: Einstein worked at the Swiss Patent Office in Bern during his annus mirabilis year.

question: Which city hosted the first modern Olympic Games in 1896?
fact: Athens hosted the first modern Olympics inspired by the ancient Greek games.

question: Which Indian industrialist flew India's first commercial flight in 1932?
fact: JRD Tata piloted the Karachi to Bombay airmail flight that became Tata Airlines.

question: Which Beatles album features the iconic Abbey Road zebra crossing on its cover?
fact: The cover photograph became one of the most recreated images in music history.

question: Which cricketer scored a century in each innings of a Test match on debut?
fact: Jamaica's Lawrence Rowe scored 214 and 100 on Test debut in 1972.

question: Which country gifted the Statue of Liberty to the United States?
fact: France presented the statue in 1886 to commemorate friendship and shared democratic ideals.

question: What was the original name of the company now known as Nintendo?
fact: Nintendo began in 1889 as Nintendo Koppai producing handmade playing cards.

question: Who was the first Formula One world champion from India?
fact: No Indian driver has yet won the Formula One World Championship.

question: Which famous explorer's ships were named Nina Pinta and Santa Maria?
fact: Columbus used the three ships on his first voyage across the Atlantic in 1492.

question: Which Indian won the Nobel Prize in Economics in 1998 for contributions to welfare economics and social choice theory?
fact: Amartya Sen's work transformed the study of poverty inequality and human development.
